title: galium humifusum subsp. besserianum
intro: Galium humifusum subsp. besserianum, also known as Bearberry, is a type of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a low-growing, woody shrub native to the mountainous regions of Europe, Asia, and North America. Traditionally, Bearberry has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a diuretic to treat urinary tract infections and other conditions such as kidney stones and bladder issues. The plant's leaves and roots can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting urinary tract health.
